bugs.title: Simplify mobile header — remove all controls and logo text
bugs.description: On mobile (≤700px), the header should be a slim, empty bar that serves as a container for contextual actions only (e.g., '+' for new list, share button on detail view). Currently it still shows the app logo, username, ThemeToggle, and Sign Out button. Username/ThemeToggle/Sign Out now live in the Settings tab. The logo text is unnecessary on mobile and wastes vertical space. Desktop header should remain unchanged (logo + username + ThemeToggle + Sign Out).
bugs.rootCause: The header JSX in App.jsx (lines 265-276) renders the logo, username, ThemeToggle, and Sign Out unconditionally with no mobile-specific conditional rendering. The `isMobile` hook is already imported but not used in the header.
reproductionSteps: Open the app on mobile (≤700px viewport) or resize browser
Observe the header — logo, username, dark mode toggle, and Sign Out button are all visible
These controls are redundant with the Settings tab, and the logo wastes vertical space
bugs.suggestedFix:
In App.jsx, conditionally render header contents based on `isMobile`: ```jsx
{!isMobile && ( <>

ShoppingListAI

{isGuest ? 'Guest' : user.displayName ?? user.email}
)}
``` On mobile the header renders as a slim empty bar. Contextual actions ('+' new list, share) can be added to this header in a follow-up or within the individual tab/detail views as they are now. Alternatively, hide the entire header on mobile via CSS if no contextual actions are needed in it yet: ```css @media (max-width: 700px) { .header { display: none; } } ``` But keeping the header element (empty) is preferred so contextual actions can be slotted in later.
acceptanceCriteria: On mobile (≤700px), the header does not show the logo text, username, ThemeToggle, or Sign Out
On mobile, the header remains as a slim bar (not removed entirely) for future contextual actions
On desktop (≥701px), the header continues to show logo, username, ThemeToggle, and Sign Out (no change)
The Settings tab on mobile still works with dark mode toggle and sign out
No extra vertical space wasted on mobile — header should be minimal height when empty
Works in both light and dark mode
bugs.notes: Combines two pieces of feedback: (1) username/ThemeToggle/Sign Out are redundant with Settings tab, (2) logo text wastes vertical space on mobile. The header bar itself should remain for future contextual actions but render empty/slim on mobile.
